2605.00583.title: Jailbreaking Vision-Language Models Through the Visual Modality
2605.00583.paper_summary: The visual modality of vision-language models (VLMs) is an underexplored attack surface for bypassing safety alignment. We introduce four jailbreak attacks exploiting the vision component: (1) encoding harmful instructions as visual symbol sequences with a decoding legend, (2) replacing harmful objects with benign substitutes (e.g., bomb -> banana) then prompting for harmful actions using the substitute term, (3) replacing harmful text in images (e.g., on book covers) with benign words while visual context preserves the original meaning, and (4) visual analogy puzzles whose solution requires inferring a prohibited concept. Evaluating across six frontier VLMs, our visual attacks bypass safety alignment and expose a cross-modality alignment gap: text-based safety training does not automatically generalize to harmful intent conveyed visually. For example, our visual cipher achieves 40.9% attack success on Claude-Haiku-4.5 versus 10.7% for an equivalent textual cipher. To further our insight into the attack mechanism, we present preliminary interpretability and mitigation results. These findings highlight that robust VLM alignment requires treating vision as a first-class target for safety post-training.
2605.00583.paper_summary_zh: 視覺語言模型（VLMs）的視覺模態是一個尚未充分探索的攻擊面，用於繞過安全對齊。我們介紹了四種利用視覺組件的越獄攻擊：（1）將有害指令編碼為帶有解碼圖例的視覺符號序列，（2）用良性替代品（例如，炸彈 -> 香蕉）替換有害物體，然後使用替代術語提示有害行為，（3）在圖像中用良性詞語替換有害文本（例如，書籍封面上的文本），同時視覺上下文保持原意，以及（4）視覺類比謎題，其解決方案需要推斷禁止的概念。在六個前沿VLM上進行評估，我們的視覺攻擊繞過了安全對齊，並暴露了跨模態對齊的差距：基於文本的安全訓練並不會自動泛化到以視覺傳達的有害意圖。例如，我們的視覺密碼在Claude-Haiku-4.5上達到40.9%的攻擊成功率，而相應的文本密碼僅為10.7%。為了進一步了解攻擊機制，我們呈現了初步的可解釋性和緩解結果。這些發現強調，穩健的VLM對齊需要將視覺視為安全後訓練的首要目標。
2605.00583.author: Aharon Azulay et.al.
2605.00583.authors: Aharon Azulay, Jan Dubiński, Zhuoyun Li, Atharv Mittal, Yossi Gandelsman
